observations.summary: The phrase 'Even if it kills me. Even if I'm ground to dust on the way. Up.' echoes the motif of physical and spiritual ascent despite extreme cost, paralleling Dan's arduous journey and sacrifices described in the narrative context.

source_evidence.quote: Up, he thought. Even if it kills me. Even if I'm ground to dust on the way. Up.

observations.target_hint: Dan's journey involves a painful ascent marked by physical and spiritual trials, including shedding earthly treasures and enduring internal conflict.

target_evidence.quote: The air, already thin and sharp at the base of Hermon, bit at Daniel’s lungs with the first step... The ground, still damp from Rafa’s cleansing tears, felt cold beneath his bare feet... His walking stick, worn smooth by countless miles, became an extension of his will.
